title: Bridge Essay: Goal-directed and habitual control in the basal ganglia: implications for Parkin

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.

**Goal-directed and habitual control in the basal ganglia: implications for Parkinson'sdisease.**. Nat Rev Neurosci.. PMID: 20944662. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2915. Progressive loss of the ascending dopaminergic projection in the basal ganglia is afundamental pathological feature of Parkinson'sdisease. Studies in animals and humans have identified spatially segregated functional territories in the basal ganglia for the control of goal-directed and habitual actions.
